title: Tapping to Honour Money as a Neutral, Loving Tool
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing moral judgement about money and building a relationship of respect
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 67 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the belief that money is morally loaded — that having too much is greedy, that wealth changes people, that wanting more makes you someone you wouldn't like. The reframe moves toward money as a neutral energy that takes its shape from how it is used.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 67 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice moral discomfort around money — when you judge others for having too much, when wanting more feels greedy, or when you worry that financial success might change who you are.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 67 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the moral weight many people carry about money — the belief that it corrupts, that having too much is greedy, that judging wealthy people is reasonable. The reframe moves toward money as a neutral tool: neither good nor bad in itself, shaped entirely by the values and intentions of the person who holds it.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once thought money was bad or greedy, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I judged people for having too much, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feared becoming someone I wouldn't like, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I've seen money as a source of conflict.
Side of eye: I thought it changed people for the worse.
Under eye: I worried it could corrupt me.
Under nose: I carried old judgements about wealth.
Chin: I'm ready to see money differently.
Collarbone: I know money itself is neutral.
Under arm: I choose to relate to it with love and respect.
Top of head: I let money be a positive force in my hands.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Money is simply energy and choice.
Side of eye: It supports what I value most.
Under eye: I use it to create and to give.
Under nose: I treat money as a trusted partner.
Chin: I honour it as a helpful, loving tool.
Collarbone: I allow wealth to reflect my highest values.
Under arm: I celebrate money's power to do good.
Top of head: I welcome money as a neutral, loving ally.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 67 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
